hero.description: Multi-cloud cost management and billing automation for GCP, AWS, and Azure resellers.

productGroups.subtitle: Group billing items into categories (e.g. Compute, Storage, Network) for clearer invoices
productGroups.name: Name

modal.addItemsDescription: Search for billing items from your cost imports. Items must be imported via "Cost Imports" first before they appear here.
modal.removeItemConfirm: Are you sure you want to remove this billing item from the group?

costImports.title: Cost Imports
costImports.subtitle: Import billing data from cloud providers. Imported data is used for invoice generation and can be categorized in "Product Groups".
costImports.noImports: No cost data imported yet. Use the API or BigQuery integration to import billing data.

reconciliation.title: Reconciliation
reconciliation.subtitle: Compare billing data with provider reports

validation.invalidEmail: Please enter a valid email address
validation.maxLength: Must be no more than {max} characters

help.title: System User Guide
help.subtitle: Sieger Management Console — module reference and workflows

flowDiagram.title: System Configuration Flow
flowDiagram.subtitle: Modules have data dependencies — you must follow this order for invoice generation to work correctly. Click any node to navigate to that module.

description.title: Why this configuration order?
description.step1: Configure data source connections first: the system needs credentials to call GCP/AWS APIs and auto-populate billing accounts and project names — preventing manual entry errors
description.step2: Create Customers next: every billing account, project, pricing rule, and invoice must belong to a customer — customers are the top-level container
description.step3: Set up Billing Accounts (GCP) or Payer Accounts (AWS) and link to customers: costs are summarized by account ID — this linkage tells the system which customer owns each account's costs
description.step4: Create Projects (GCP) or Linked Accounts (AWS) under billing/payer accounts: cost data is tagged by project/account ID — forming the complete attribution chain
description.step5: Configure Product Groups and Pricing Lists: groups turn hundreds of SKU lines into readable categories; pricing lists define the reseller price charged to customers
description.step6: Import cost data: with projects/accounts and product groups in place, every cost record can be matched to its customer and category
description.step7: Run Invoice Batches: the system automatically applies pricing rules and credits per customer, generating draft invoices for the entire billing month in one go
description.step8: Review, lock, send via EDM, and record payments: locking prevents accidental changes; EDM sends invoices in one click; recording payments closes the AR cycle

quickStart.title: Quick Start
quickStart.description: Follow this order — each step has dependencies on the previous ones:
quickStart.step1: (Super admin) Go to Operations > GCP Source Data to add service account credentials and BigQuery config (for AWS, configure AWS credentials and CUR path)
quickStart.step2: Go to Customer Management > Customers to create a customer — fill in company name, internal ID, contact email, and finance email
quickStart.step3: In the customer detail page, create an Opportunity — link a Vendor (GCP/AWS), pricing list, and account list
quickStart.step4: Add billing/payer accounts: GCP Billing Account IDs or AWS Payer Account IDs — link them to the corresponding customer
quickStart.step5: Set up projects/linked accounts: GCP Projects or AWS Linked Accounts linked to billing/payer accounts and customers
quickStart.step6: Configure product groups and pricing lists — groups define invoice categories, pricing lists define discount rules; together they determine invoice content and amounts
quickStart.step7: Import cost data — ensure projects/accounts and product groups are configured before importing raw billing data
quickStart.step8: Generate and manage invoices: run invoice batch → review drafts → lock invoices → send via EDM → record payments

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.description: The default landing page showing key business metrics and summaries. Content adapts automatically based on user role and data permissions — different roles see different scopes of data.
dashboard.step1: After login, you land on the Dashboard showing a business overview matching your role and customer scope
dashboard.step2: Super Admins see global data summaries; other roles only see data within their authorized customer scope
dashboard.step3: Use the top filters to switch between different months or dimensions

reports.title: Reports
reports.description: (Planned) Customizable report center with multi-dimensional analytics. Report types include: AWS/GCP customer usage, untagged LinkedID/ProjectID usage, AWS SP coverage & utilization, GCP CUD coverage & utilization, and contract completion statistics.
reports.step1: Select a report type: AWS customer usage, GCP customer usage, SP/CUD coverage analysis, contract completion, etc.
reports.step2: Reports automatically filter data by role, ensuring each user only sees authorized data
reports.step3: Export reports as Excel/PDF for distribution and archival

users.title: User Management (Super Admin)
users.description: Controls who can log in and what data they can see. Data isolation is enforced through "Customer Scopes" — this is the core multi-tenancy mechanism.
users.step1: Click "Create" — fill in basic info: first name, last name, email, and login password
users.step2: Select a role — the right panel dynamically shows a detailed description and permission list for the selected role, helping you understand the differences
users.step3: For non-super_admin roles, set the "Customer Access" scope — select which customer this user can access; once set, the backend automatically filters so the user only sees that customer's invoices, projects, and billing data
users.step4: After creation, click "Manage" to go to the user detail page where you can adjust roles, scopes, or deactivate the account
users.step5: When someone leaves or their role changes, promptly deactivate or adjust their customer scope — the list shows each user's last login time and status (active with green icon / inactive with red icon)

auditLogs.title: Audit Logs
auditLogs.description: Every data change is recorded: who did what, and when, including complete before/after data snapshots. Used for compliance audits, incident investigation, and evidence in billing disputes.
auditLogs.step1: Use filters to narrow your search by action type (CREATE/UPDATE/DELETE/LOCK/BIND etc.), resource type (customers/invoices/projects etc.), and date range
auditLogs.step2: Click the "Details" button on any log entry to see the full record — including timestamp, operator, IP address, and a JSON diff showing before and after data
auditLogs.step3: Regularly review high-privilege operations (DELETE shown in red, LOCK in gray) to ensure no unauthorized changes — CREATE is shown in green for easy visual distinction

invoiceManagement.title: Invoice Management
invoiceManagement.description: Unified invoice management for all customers. Displays unlocked invoices by Invoice Month, organized by customer, with both Invoice and detailed statements. Supports regional invoice templates (Mainland China / HK / Singapore / Japan) and EDM email integration for one-click billing.
invoiceManagement.step1: View all unlocked invoices by Invoice Month — displayed by customer, each containing an Invoice section and a detailed statement section
invoiceManagement.step2: Mainland China customers: upload the issued invoice file directly
invoiceManagement.step3: HK / Singapore / Japan customers: select the contracting entity → select customer name (auto-fills entity, address, email) → enter Service Description, Amount, Balance of Prepayment, Subtotal, Tax → system auto-generates the Invoice
invoiceManagement.step4: Manage detailed statements: GCP statements default to Excel, AWS statements default to PDF; Phase 1 supports direct upload, Phase 2 will auto-generate from monthly billing calculations; custom statements can also be uploaded
invoiceManagement.step5: Use EDM for one-click sending: customize email templates, select customer, and send invoices with detailed statements to the customer's finance email

balanceManagement.title: Balance & Top-up Management
balanceManagement.description: (Planned) Manages pre-paid customer account balances and post-paid customer payment terms. Automatically alerts for overdue post-paid customers and underfunded pre-paid customers to prevent bad debt.
balanceManagement.step1: View the alert panel: overdue post-paid customers and underfunded pre-paid customers are auto-highlighted
balanceManagement.step2: Pre-paid balance management: select customer → perform manual top-up or balance adjustment → changes are automatically linked to the customer account with audit trail
balanceManagement.step3: Post-paid payment progress: select the corresponding invoice → update payment progress status → system auto-calculates whether terms are exceeded
balanceManagement.step4: All balance changes and top-ups are automatically logged in audit logs for financial traceability

paymentProgress.title: Payment Progress
paymentProgress.description: Records actual customer payments, tracks payment progress per invoice, and closes the accounts receivable cycle. Supports partial payments and multiple payment methods.
paymentProgress.step1: Click "Record Payment" — select the unpaid invoice from the dropdown showing invoice number, customer name, and outstanding balance
paymentProgress.step2: The system displays invoice details (total, paid, remaining) and pre-fills the payment amount with the remaining balance — modify for partial payments
paymentProgress.step3: Enter payment date (defaults to today), select payment method (Bank Transfer / Credit Card / Check / Wire / ACH / Other), enter reference number and notes
paymentProgress.step4: After submission, invoice status updates automatically; summary cards show real-time total payment count and amount

reconciliation.title: Reconciliation
reconciliation.description: Compares system-calculated invoice amounts against cloud provider raw billing data at the customer level, showing discrepancies to help catch and fix issues before invoices are sent.
reconciliation.step1: Select the month to reconcile and click "Generate Report" — the system compares raw cost totals against invoice totals for that month
reconciliation.step2: Review the 4 summary cards: raw cost total (with record count), invoice total (with invoice count), variance amount (with percentage — green = over, red = under), and status ("Reconciled" in green if variance ≤ 1%, otherwise "Needs Review" in orange)
reconciliation.step3: Check the per-customer breakdown table — each row shows customer name, raw cost, invoiced amount, and variance; green checkmark = matched, orange/red arrows = overcharged or undercharged
reconciliation.step4: If there are unassigned projects, an orange warning appears — listing projects not linked to any customer along with their cost totals. Go to Project Management to complete the linking, then re-reconcile

customers.title: Customer Management
customers.description: Customers are the top-level entity. Each customer contains Basic Information and detail tabs (Opportunities, Contracts, Credits, Invoices). All business data is organized around customers.
customers.step1: Click "Create" to fill in basic info: company name (required), internal ID (e.g. 202602241), contact email, finance email
customers.step2: After creation, enter the customer detail page to manage associated data through 4 tabs
customers.step3: Opportunities tab: create business opportunities — fill in Opp name (e.g. GCP-CDN), Opp ID (e.g. GCP-CUST-001), select Vendor (GCP/AWS), link pricing list and account list; status can be changed in edit mode (Active/Suspended/Terminated)
customers.step4: Contracts tab: manage this customer's contracts; Credits tab: view and manage this customer's credit balances
customers.step5: Invoices tab: view all invoices and payment status for this customer — edit mode allows changing customer status (Active/Suspended/Terminated)

contracts.title: Contract Management
contracts.description: (Planned) Contract import and management. Supports uploading contract files, recording terms, validity periods, committed spend, and linking to customers and opportunities.
contracts.step1: Upload contract files and fill in basic info: contract number, start/end dates, committed spend, associated vendor
contracts.step2: Link the contract to the corresponding customer and Opportunity — the system automatically establishes the association
contracts.step3: Contract completion data feeds into the "Contract Completion Statistics" report, helping track contract fulfillment

pricingLists.title: Pricing Lists
pricingLists.description: Defines the reseller prices charged to customers. Set discount rates, fixed unit prices, or tiered pricing per product group, and assign to customer Opportunities to take effect during invoice generation.
pricingLists.step1: Click "Create" — select the customer and enter a name (e.g. "Standard 2025"). The customer cannot be changed after creation
pricingLists.step2: After creation, click "Manage Rules" to enter the detail page — add pricing rules per product group here
pricingLists.step3: When adding a rule, choose the type: "Percentage Discount" (e.g. 10% off = rate 0.90), "Fixed Unit Price", or "Tiered Pricing" (different rates based on spend thresholds)
pricingLists.step4: For tiered pricing: click "Add Tier" to set usage ranges (from/to) with corresponding rates or unit prices — leave the last tier's "to" empty for unlimited
pricingLists.step5: Link the pricing list in the customer's Opportunity — invoice runs look up the Opportunity's pricing list to calculate final amounts per vendor dimension

credits.title: Credits
credits.description: Customers may have pre-paid deposits, promotional credits, or contractual balances. Credits are automatically deducted from invoice totals during invoice runs — no manual calculation needed. The system tracks remaining balance per credit.
credits.step1: Click "Add Credit" — select a customer and choose the type: Promotional, Commitment, Goodwill, or Refund
credits.step2: Enter the amount (to the cent), validity dates (defaults to today through one year), and a description — amount and dates are required
credits.step3: Credits take effect automatically during invoice runs: the system matches currently valid credits by date range, deducts from the invoice total, and switches to the next valid credit when one is exhausted
credits.step4: Check the "Remaining" column in the list — green indicates available balance, gray means fully used; expired or zero-balance credits are automatically marked Inactive

specialRules.title: Special Rules
specialRules.description: Configure price adjustment rules for specific customers or scenarios that standard pricing lists can't cover, such as one-time discounts for specific SKUs or promotional offers.
specialRules.step1: Create a special rule specifying the applicable customer, SKU, or product group scope — supports overrides down to individual SKU level
specialRules.step2: Set the rule type and priority — special rules take precedence over general rules in pricing lists, ensuring special cases are handled correctly
specialRules.step3: Set a validity period — expired special rules are automatically deactivated and won't affect subsequent invoice calculations

rawCostImports.title: Raw Cost Imports
rawCostImports.description: Invoice generation requires raw cost data. Data is read from GCP BigQuery / AWS CUR and other sources, with each record containing project ID, SKU, usage, and cost. This page is a read-only view showing all import batch statuses and summaries.
rawCostImports.step1: Prerequisites: ensure projects/linked accounts and product groups are configured — cost records are matched to customers via project/account ID and to categories via SKU
rawCostImports.step2: Ensure the data source connection has BigQuery/CUR paths and credentials correctly configured — these are the sources from which the system reads raw billing data
rawCostImports.step3: The summary cards at the top show total batches, total rows, and total cost — giving a quick overview of the overall import status
rawCostImports.step4: Use the month and source filters to view specific import records — each record shows import time, row count, total cost, status (COMPLETED in green / FAILED in red), and who imported it

gcpSourceData.title: GCP Source Data
gcpSourceData.description: Configure GCP service account credentials and BigQuery connection info (Super Admin only). The system uses these to call GCP Cloud Billing API and Resource Manager API, with per-customer connections enforcing API-level data isolation.
gcpSourceData.step1: Click "Add Connection" — paste the GCP service account JSON; the system auto-extracts client_email and private_key, no manual entry needed
gcpSourceData.step2: Fill in BigQuery config: Project ID, Dataset name, and table name — these are required for cost imports to read GCP billing data
gcpSourceData.step3: Click "Test" to verify — the test calls the GCP Billing API and on success shows accessible billing accounts; on failure shows the specific error
gcpSourceData.step4: In Customer Management, bind this connection to the customer — once bound, that customer's API calls use their own credentials, ensuring multi-customer data isolation

gcpBillingAccounts.title: GCP Billing Accounts
gcpBillingAccounts.description: GCP charges are summarized at the billing account level. Recording which billing accounts belong to which customer is the foundation of correct cost attribution. A customer can have multiple billing accounts.
gcpBillingAccounts.step1: Click "Create" — enter the GCP billing account ID (format: XXXXXX-XXXXXX-XXXXXX, found in GCP Console → Billing)
gcpBillingAccounts.step2: Alternatively, use "Import from GCP" — the system uses credentials to auto-list available billing accounts; click to import
gcpBillingAccounts.step3: Link the billing account to a customer — when cost data is imported, the system uses this link to attribute charges correctly
gcpBillingAccounts.step4: Verify the link is correct — an incorrect association means invoices go to the wrong customer

gcpProjects.title: GCP Projects
gcpProjects.description: GCP cost data is tagged by project ID. Projects must be linked to a billing account and a customer to form the complete attribution chain (Project → Billing Account → Customer) for costs to be correctly assigned.
gcpProjects.step1: Option 1 — Manual: enter the GCP project ID and wait ~1 second; the system auto-calls the Resource Manager API to fetch the project name and number
gcpProjects.step2: Option 2 — Import from GCP: select a billing account → click "Load Projects" → the system lists all projects → click "Import" to add
gcpProjects.step3: Link the project to a billing account — clarifies which billing account bears this project's costs
gcpProjects.step4: Assign to a customer — completes the chain Project → Billing Account → Customer; invoice runs use this to aggregate costs
gcpProjects.step5: When editing, fill in IAM Role (optional) and click "Sync from GCP" to refresh the project name — names are always read-only, sourced from official GCP data

gcpSkuGroups.title: GCP SKU Groups
gcpSkuGroups.description: GCP bills contain hundreds of SKUs. Displaying them all on an invoice is unreadable. SKU groups map SKUs into human-readable categories like "Compute" or "Storage", and invoices are summarized by group.
gcpSkuGroups.step1: Click "Create" — enter a group code (auto-uppercased, e.g. COMPUTE), display name (e.g. "Compute Resources"), and description
gcpSkuGroups.step2: After creation, click "Manage Items" to enter the detail page — add SKUs to this group here
gcpSkuGroups.step3: Add via "Browse by Category": use the 4-level cascade selector — Service → Resource Family → Resource Group → Usage Type — then check the SKUs to add
gcpSkuGroups.step4: Add via "Keyword Search": enter a keyword (e.g. e2-standard, Cloud Storage) to search matching SKUs directly
gcpSkuGroups.step5: SKU groups must be configured before importing cost data — the import matches each SKU to its group; ungrouped SKU costs can't be categorized on invoices

gcpRegistrations.title: GCP Registrations
gcpRegistrations.description: (Planned) Manage GCP customer reseller registration records, track registration status, and link to billing accounts.
gcpRegistrations.step1: Record GCP account reseller registration info and approval status
gcpRegistrations.step2: Registration records are auto-linked to billing accounts for traceability

gcpContracts.title: GCP Contracts
gcpContracts.description: (Planned) Manage GCP-related vendor contracts, including reseller agreement terms and committed use discount (CUD) commitments.
gcpContracts.step1: Enter GCP vendor contract info: contract number, committed spend, discount terms, and validity period
gcpContracts.step2: Contract data feeds into reseller cost and margin calculations, linked to CUD coverage analysis in reports

gcpCredits.title: GCP Credits
gcpCredits.description: (Planned) Manage vendor-level credits from GCP, such as promotional credits and partner rewards. Distinct from customer-level credits in Customer Management.
gcpCredits.step1: Enter GCP-granted credits: type, amount, validity period, and applicable conditions
gcpCredits.step2: Credits are automatically matched and deducted during cost calculations, reducing actual reseller costs

gcpFundings.title: GCP Fundings
gcpFundings.description: (Planned) Manage GCP funding programs such as CUD committed use discount purchases and partner fund investments.
gcpFundings.step1: Record GCP Funding plans: type, amount, commitment period, and usage conditions
gcpFundings.step2: Track funding utilization progress and remaining balance

awsSourceData.title: AWS Source Data
awsSourceData.description: (Planned) Configure AWS account credentials and Cost & Usage Report (CUR) data sources for the system to access AWS billing data.
awsSourceData.step1: Configure AWS IAM credentials or Role ARN, set the CUR S3 bucket path and report format
awsSourceData.step2: Test the connection to ensure AWS billing data can be read successfully

awsPayerAccounts.title: AWS Payer Accounts
awsPayerAccounts.description: (Planned) Manage AWS Organizations payer accounts. Payer accounts are the top-level billing entity in AWS consolidated billing — all linked account charges roll up to the payer.
awsPayerAccounts.step1: Add the AWS payer account ID and link to a customer
awsPayerAccounts.step2: Configure the Cost & Usage Report S3 path and report format
awsPayerAccounts.step3: The payer-to-customer link determines cost attribution — similar to GCP billing accounts

awsLinkedAccounts.title: AWS Linked Accounts
awsLinkedAccounts.description: (Planned) Manage AWS member accounts under payer accounts. Similar to GCP Projects — cost data is categorized by linked account.
awsLinkedAccounts.step1: Add AWS linked account IDs and bind them to a payer account
awsLinkedAccounts.step2: Assign linked accounts to a customer — establishing the chain: Linked Account → Payer → Customer
awsLinkedAccounts.step3: Supports auto-importing linked account lists from the AWS Organizations API

awsOpportunities.title: AWS Opportunities
awsOpportunities.description: (Planned) Manage AWS-related business opportunities, tracking Savings Plans, Reserved Instances, and other optimization initiatives.
awsOpportunities.step1: Create AWS opportunities: link to customer, record SP/RI purchase plans and expected savings
awsOpportunities.step2: Track implementation progress and actual savings rates, linked to SP coverage analysis in reports

awsContracts.title: AWS Contracts
awsContracts.description: (Planned) Manage AWS-related vendor contracts, including EDP (Enterprise Discount Program) agreements.
awsContracts.step1: Enter AWS vendor contract info: contract number, EDP discount terms, committed spend, and validity period
awsContracts.step2: Contract data feeds into reseller cost and margin calculations

awsCredits.title: AWS Credits
awsCredits.description: (Planned) Manage vendor-level credits from AWS, such as APN partner credits and PoC credits.
awsCredits.step1: Enter AWS-granted credits: type, amount, validity period, and applicable conditions
awsCredits.step2: Credits are automatically matched and deducted during cost calculations

awsFundings.title: AWS Fundings
awsFundings.description: (Planned) Manage AWS funding programs such as MDF (Market Development Funds) and partner investments.
awsFundings.step1: Record AWS Funding plans: type, amount, usage conditions, and reimbursement requirements
awsFundings.step2: Track funding utilization progress and reimbursement status

roles.title: Role Permissions
roles.superAdmin: Full system access — can manage data source connections, users, and all customer data with no scope restrictions. Limit to 1-2 accounts per system.
roles.admin: Administrative access within assigned customer scopes — can manage customers, billing accounts, projects, and invoices, but only for their assigned customers.
roles.finance: Finance team access — can view invoices, record payments, manage credits and balances, but cannot modify customer configuration or system settings.
roles.viewer: Read-only access to data within assigned scopes. Suitable for customer portal accounts where the customer just needs to view their own invoices.

tips.title: Best Practices
tips.tip1: Never enter project/account names manually — the system auto-fetches them from GCP/AWS to ensure they match official names exactly
tips.tip2: If you find an error after locking an invoice, re-run the invoice batch to generate a corrected version rather than unlocking the original
tips.tip3: Create a separate data source connection per customer and bind it in Customer Management — this is the correct way to ensure multi-customer API data isolation
tips.tip4: Assign Customer Scopes (not admin roles) to customer-facing users — this ensures they only see their own data, enforced automatically by the backend
tips.tip5: Configure product groups / SKU groups before importing cost data — without them, imported costs will be uncategorized and cannot be properly grouped on invoices
tips.tip6: Use the Opportunity feature to manage each customer's cloud vendor relationships (GCP/AWS) separately, making it easy to independently track pricing, contracts, and accounts per vendor

gcpConnections.title: GCP Connections
gcpConnections.subtitle: Manage GCP service accounts and API keys for accessing the Cloud Billing API
gcpConnections.noConnections: No GCP connections yet. Click the button below to add your first connection.

modal.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this GCP connection? This action cannot be undone.

placeholders.name: e.g. Production Main Account
placeholders.pasteJson: Paste the full service account JSON — client_email and private_key will be extracted automatically
placeholders.privateKeyEdit: Leave blank to keep the existing key
placeholders.apiKeyEdit: Leave blank to keep the existing key
placeholders.billingProjectId: GCP project ID where BigQuery dataset resides

hints.group: Groups help you organize multiple connections. Type an existing group name or create a new one.
hints.pasteJsonHint: You can paste the entire service account JSON file — the required fields will be extracted automatically.
hints.privateKeyEdit: Leave blank to keep the current private key unchanged.
hints.apiKeyEdit: Leave blank to keep the current API key unchanged.
hints.billingConfig: Configure BigQuery billing export to enable automatic cost data fetching. Requires Billing Export → BigQuery enabled in GCP Console.
hints.billingAccountIds: Optional. Leave empty to query all billing accounts.

gcpConnections.billingConfigDesc: Enable automatic billing data fetching from BigQuery
